results.question: Show all students
results.baseline_sql:
SELECT * 
FROM students;

results.question: List all courses
results.baseline_sql:
SELECT *

results.system_sql: SELECT id, code, title, credits FROM courses;

results.question: Top scoring student
results.baseline_sql:
SELECT s.name, AVG(sc.marks) AS average_marks
FROM students s
JOIN scores sc ON s.id = sc.student_id
GROUP BY s.id, s.name
ORDER BY average_marks DESC
LIMIT 1;
results.system_sql: SELECT s.name FROM students s INNER JOIN scores sc ON s.id = sc.student_id ORDER BY sc.marks DESC LIMIT 1;

results.question: Show student name with course and marks
results.baseline_sql:
SELECT s.name, c.title, sc.marks
FROM students s
JOIN scores sc ON s.id = sc.student_id
JOIN courses c ON sc.course_id = c.id;
results.system_sql:
SELECT s.name, c.title, sc.marks 
FROM students s 
JOIN scores sc ON s.id = sc.student_id 
JOIN courses c ON sc.course_id = c.id;

results.question: List students and their courses
results.baseline_sql:
SELECT 
  s.name AS student_name,
  c.title AS course_title,
  s.age AS student_age,
  c.credits AS course_credits
FROM 
  students s
  JOIN scores sc ON s.id = sc.student_id
  JOIN courses c ON sc.course_id = c.id
ORDER BY 
  s.name, c.title;
results.system_sql:
SELECT s.name, c.title 
FROM students s 
JOIN scores sc ON s.id = sc.student_id 
JOIN courses c ON sc.course_id = c.id;

results.question: Average marks per student
results.baseline_sql:
SELECT 
    s.name, 
    AVG(sc.marks) AS average_marks
FROM 
    students s
JOIN 
    scores sc ON s.id = sc.student_id
GROUP BY 
    s.name;
results.system_sql: SELECT name, AVG(marks) FROM students INNER JOIN scores ON students.id = scores.student_id GROUP BY students.id;

results.question: Top 3 students by marks
results.baseline_sql:
SELECT s.name, AVG(sc.marks) as average_marks
FROM students s
JOIN scores sc ON s.id = sc.student_id
GROUP BY s.name
ORDER BY average_marks DESC
LIMIT 3;
results.system_sql:
SELECT s.name, SUM(sc.marks) AS total_marks 
FROM students s 
JOIN scores sc ON s.id = sc.student_id 
GROUP BY s.name 
ORDER BY total_marks DESC 
LIMIT 3;

CREATE TABLE students_backup AS
SELECT id, name AS student_name, email, age, department

results.system_sql: SELECT students.name AS student_name FROM students;

results.question: student_scores instead of scores
results.baseline_sql:
-- Create a new table with the same structure as scores
CREATE TABLE student_scores (
  id INT PRIMARY KEY,
  student_id INT,
  course_id INT,
  marks DECIMAL(5, 2),
  exam_date DATE,
  FOREIGN KEY (student_id) REFERENCES students(id),
  FOREIGN KEY (course_id) REFERENCES courses(id)
);
results.system_sql:
SELECT s.id, s.name, c.code, c.title, sc.marks, sc.exam_date 
FROM students s 
JOIN scores sc ON s.id = sc.student_id 
JOIN courses c ON sc.course_id = c.id;

results.question: List student emails with course names and marks above average
results.baseline_sql:
SELECT 
    s.email, 
    c.title AS course_name, 
    sc.marks
FROM 
    students s
JOIN 
    scores sc ON s.id = sc.student_id
JOIN 
    courses c ON sc.course_id = c.id
WHERE 
    sc.marks > (SELECT AVG(marks) FROM scores WHERE course_id = c.id)
ORDER BY 
    c.title, 
    s.email;
results.system_error: SQL generation failed: Groq request failed. Check network, API key, and model name.

results.question: Students who performed better than average in each course
results.baseline_sql:
WITH course_avg AS (
  SELECT c.id, c.code, c.title, AVG(s.marks) AS avg_marks
  FROM scores s
  JOIN courses c ON s.course_id = c.id
  GROUP BY c.id, c.code, c.title
)
SELECT s.name, s.email, c.code, c.title, s.marks
FROM scores s
JOIN courses c ON s.course_id = c.id
JOIN course_avg ca ON c.id = ca.id
WHERE s.marks > ca.avg_marks;
results.system_error: SQL generation failed: Groq request failed. Check network, API key, and model name.

results.question: Find students who scored above average in at least 2 courses
results.baseline_sql:
WITH student_scores AS (
  SELECT 
    s.id, 
    s.name, 
    s.email, 
    s.age, 
    s.department, 
    AVG(sc.marks) AS avg_score
  FROM 
    students s
  JOIN 
    scores sc ON s.id = sc.student_id
  GROUP BY 
    s.id, s.name, s.email, s.age, s.department
),
student_above_avg AS (
  SELECT 
    id, 
    name, 
    email, 
    age, 
    department, 
    avg_score
  FROM 
    student_scores
  WHERE 
    avg_score > (SELECT AVG(marks) FROM scores)
)
SELECT 
  id, 
  name, 
  email, 
  age, 
  department
FROM 
  student_above_avg
GROUP BY 
  id, name, email, age, department
HAVING 
  COUNT(*) >= 2;
results.system_error: SQL generation failed: Groq request failed. Check network, API key, and model name.

results.question: List students whose average marks are above the overall average
results.system_sql:
SELECT name, email, AVG(marks) AS avg_marks 
FROM scores 
JOIN students ON scores.student_id = students.id 
GROUP BY scores.student_id 
HAVING AVG(marks) > (SELECT AVG(marks) FROM scores);

results.question: Count students scoring above 90 in each course
results.baseline_sql:
SELECT 
    c.code, 
    c.title, 
    COUNT(s.student_id) AS students_above_90
FROM 
    scores s
JOIN 
    courses c ON s.course_id = c.id
WHERE 
    s.marks > 90
GROUP BY 
    c.code, 
    c.title
ORDER BY 
    c.code;
